title: Document advisor lifecycle status tracking in registry.json
perceive: The advisor registry tracks advisor status as 'stub' but lacks explicit documentation of activation states (development vs. production). This creates ambiguity about whether stub status is permanent or a pre-activation state.
execute: 1. Update WAI-Spoke/advisors/registry.json schema documentation to define status values: stub (pre-activation), active (production), deprecated (retired).
2. Add activation_date field to the registry.json schema to track when advisors transition to active status.
3. Document the advisory lifecycle state machine in WAI-Spoke/reference/ for future reference.
verify: WAI-Spoke/advisors/registry.json schema documentation exists and clearly defines advisor lifecycle states and activation path.
acceptance_criteria: registry.json has documented schema with status enum values
activation_date field is present in registry entries
Lifecycle state machine documented in reference/
